title: Learning to Recover Task Experts from a Multi-Task Merged Model
abstract: arXiv:2606.26902v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multi-task model merging aims to consolidate several task-specific experts into a unified model, yet static merging consistently suffers from parameter interference. While dynamic merging models aim to bridge this gap, many works rely on the costly storage and loading of redundant expert components at inference. In this work, from the perspective of task expert, we view parameter interference as parameter perturbation introduced to each expert during merging process. We show that such parameter perturbations can be modeled as affine transformation, which can be approximated as additive offsets. Motivated by these, we propose Recover Task eXpert (ReTeX), a framework that predicts those offsets, in order to undo parameter interference and recover task-expert performance from a single merged checkpoint. To recover the appropriate expert when task identity is unknown, we introduce a router-free task identifier based on SVD subspace signature

Title: Learning to Recover Task Experts from a Multi-Task Merged Model
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26902v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multi-task model merging aims to consolidate several task-specific experts into a unified model, yet static merging consistently suffers from parameter interference. While dynamic merging models aim to bridge this gap, many works rely on the costly storage and loading of redundant expert components at inference. In this work, from the perspective of task expert, we view parameter interference as parameter perturbation introduced to each expert during merging process. We show that such parameter perturbations can be modeled as affine transformation, which can be approximated as additive offsets. Motivated by these, we propose Recover Task eXpert (ReTeX), a framework that predicts those offsets, in order to undo parameter interference and recover task-expert performance from a single merged checkpoint. To recover the appropriate expert when task identity is unknown, we introduce a router-free task identifier based on SVD subspace signature
translated_title: 从多任务合并模型中恢复任务专家
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26902v1 公告类型：新 摘要：多任务模型合并旨在将多个特定任务的专家模型整合到一个统一模型中，但静态合并始终存在参数干扰问题。虽然动态合并模型试图弥补这一差距，但许多方法在推理时需要昂贵地存储和加载冗余的专家组件。在这项工作中，从任务专家的视角出发，我们将参数干扰视为合并过程中对每个专家引入的参数扰动。我们证明这种参数扰动可以建模为仿射变换，并可近似为加性偏移。受此启发，我们提出恢复任务专家（ReTeX）框架，该框架预测这些偏移量，以消除参数干扰并从单个合并检查点恢复任务专家的性能。当任务身份未知时，我们引入一种基于SVD子空间签名的无路由器任务识别器。
tldr: 提出ReTeX框架，通过预测参数干扰的加性偏移量，从单个多任务合并检查点中恢复各任务专家的性能，并引入基于SVD子空间签名的无路由器任务识别方法。
en_tdlr: Propose the ReTeX framework that recovers task-expert performance from a single multi-task merged checkpoint by predicting additive offsets of parameter interference, and introduces a router-free task identifier based on SVD subspace signature.
